channel.title: Caching on Docs
channel.description: Recent content in Caching on Docs

item.title: Chapter 1, Part 3 – REST Caching
item.description:
What Is API Caching?

Caching in the REST communication model means storing a copy of a response at some intermediate point — client or server side — so that repeated requests for the same resource do not require a full round trip to the origin server.

Caching is one of REST’s core performance advantages. It reduces latency, lowers server load, and makes applications more responsive under repeated or concurrent requests.
